Name: Zamtel Infrastructure Bond 2022A – 4G/LTE Rollout (ZMW 450 million)
Description: 7-year fixed-rate bond issued by Zambia Telecommunications Corporation (Zamtel) on the Lusaka Securities Exchange. Coupon 14.0% p.a. Proceeds finance nationwide 4G/LTE network expansion to 1,200 additional base stations in rural districts. GRZ step-in guarantee through IDC. SECZ prospectus approval SEC/ZB/2022/0078. ZICTA spectrum licence ZIC/4G/2021/001.

Name: Zamtel Infrastructure Bond 2023A – Fibre Expansion (ZMW 380 million)
Description: 5-year bond, Coupon 13.5% p.a. LuSE-listed. Proceeds fund 3,200 km national fibre optic backbone expansion connecting provincial capitals, border crossings and universities. Backed by Zamtel fibre asset base and GRZ guarantee. SECZ ref SEC/ZB/2023/0045. AfDB co-funding: USD 12M grant for rural fibre.

Name: Zamtel 5G Transition Bond 2024 (ZMW 620 million) – ZICTA Approved
Description: 10-year bond for Zamtel's 5G NR (New Radio) spectrum licence acquisition and core network modernisation. Coupon 15.5% p.a. Spectrum licensed by ZICTA under 3.5 GHz band licence ZIC/5G/2024/001. Proceeds also cover Open RAN (O-RAN) deployment in Lusaka, Kitwe, Ndola, Livingstone. SECZ ref SEC/ZB/2024/0022.

Name: Zamtel Rural Connectivity Bond 2024 (ZMW 280 million) – USAF Funded
Description: 6-year bond co-financed with the Universal Service Access Fund (USAF) for connecting 850 un-served schools, clinics and community centres across Northern, Luapula and North-Western provinces. Coupon 12.5% p.a. ZICTA USO obligation funding. AfDB/EU funding: USD 8M grant complement. SECZ ref SEC/ZSB/2024/0033.

Name: Zamtel National Data Centre Bond 2025 (ZMW 520 million)
Description: 8-year bond for construction of Zamtel's Tier III National Data Centre at Lusaka North Technology Park (22,000 sq ft, 5 MW IT load). Coupon 16.0% p.a. Facility will host GRZ e-government services, Smart Zambia Institute cloud and Zamtel enterprise customers. SECZ ref SEC/ZB/2025/0006. IDC equity co-investment: ZMW 130M.

Name: Zamtel Equity Token – IDC/GRZ 100% Government Holding
Description: Tokenised equity representing the Government of Zambia's 100% ownership of Zamtel through the Industrial Development Corporation (IDC). Total net asset value based on Zamtel Q4 2024 audited balance sheet: total assets ZMW 4.2B, net equity ZMW 1.8B. IDC portfolio valuation applying 6× EBITDA multiple to FY2024 earnings. IDC ref IDC-ZAMTEL-2024.

Name: Zamtel Mobile Financial Services Subsidiary Equity Token (40% stake)
Description: Tokenised equity representing Zamtel's 40% strategic stake in ZamPay Mobile Financial Services Ltd – Zambia's emerging mobile money platform. Zamtel provides USSD infrastructure, MNO network and brand. BoZ EMI licence EMI/2023/0012. JV partner: African Payment Technology Holdings (APTH, 60%). Valued at 4× annual transaction fee revenue.

Name: ZamTower Infrastructure JV Equity Token (51% stake)
Description: Tokenised equity representing Zamtel's 51% controlling stake in ZamTower Infrastructure Ltd – a tower sharing JV owning 1,200 passive infrastructure sites across Zambia. JV partner: IHS Towers (49%). Tower rental revenue: ZMW 180M/yr from MTN, Airtel and Zamtel tenants. Valued at 10× annual EBITDA. ZICTA tower sharing approval ZIC-TS-2022.

Name: Zamtel e-Government Cloud Services Equity Token (60% stake)
Description: Tokenised equity representing Zamtel's 60% controlling stake in the Smart Zambia Cloud Platform JV operating GRZ e-government cloud infrastructure. JV partner: Huawei Technologies (40%). Annual government SLA revenue: ZMW 240M. Contract term: 2022–2030. Smart Zambia Institute contract ref SZI/CLOUD/2022/001. IDC oversight.

Name: Zamtel 4G/LTE 800 MHz Spectrum Concession – ZICTA National Licence
Description: Tokenised spectrum concession right for Zamtel's 4G/LTE 800 MHz band (2×10 MHz FDD) national spectrum licence. Licence term: 2020–2040 (20 years, renewable). Annual spectrum fees: ZMW 42M. Coverage obligation: 70% of population by Year 5. ZICTA licence ZIC/4G/2020/002. Value based on 15-year spectrum NPV at 12% discount rate.

Name: Zamtel 5G NR 3.5 GHz Spectrum Concession (2×100 MHz TDD)
Description: Tokenised 5G NR spectrum concession right: 100 MHz TDD band in 3.4–3.8 GHz range. ZICTA licence ZIC/5G/2024/001, initial term 15 years (2024–2039). Launch obligation: 5G services in Lusaka and Kitwe by December 2025. Annual spectrum fee: ZMW 68M. First 5G licence issued in Zambia. Value represents strategic future competitive advantage and infrastructure NPV.

Name: Zamtel National Fibre Right-of-Way Concession (3,200 km)
Description: Tokenised right-of-way concession for Zamtel's 3,200 km national fibre optic backbone along Zambia Railway and TAZARA rail corridors. Concession granted by Ministry of Infrastructure under Infrastructure Act 2018. Term: 50 years (2010–2060). Annual ROW fee: ZMW 28M. Allows installation, maintenance and commercial operation of fibre duct and OPGW cable.

Name: Zamtel Lusaka Underground Duct and Conduit Concession
Description: Tokenised municipal concession right to operate and lease Zamtel's underground duct and conduit network in Lusaka City: 840 km of ducts running under major arterials. Lusaka City Council concession agreement LCC/INFRA/2015/004, renewed 2025–2040. Annual fee: ZMW 18M. Ducts leased to competing operators at regulated rates under ZICTA open access directive OAD-2023.

Name: Zamtel–Alibaba Cloud National Data Centre PPP Token (ZMW 1.8 billion)
Description: PPP token for the GRZ-approved 20-year Build-Operate-Transfer data centre concession between Zamtel and Alibaba Cloud (Aliyun). Facility: 5,000 sq m, 10 MW IT load at Lusaka North Tech Park. GRZ mandates Alibaba Cloud to host Smart Zambia workloads (eGov, land records, NHIMA). ZPPA approval ZPPA/PPP/ICT/2024/005. Financial close Q3 2025.

Name: Zamtel Last-Mile Rural Connectivity PPP – North-Western Province
Description: PPP token for USAF-funded Last Mile Connectivity programme in North-Western Province. 180 community Wi-Fi hotspots and 45 school fibre connections in Mwinilunga, Ikelenge, Kasempa, Mufumbwe, Kabompo and Chavuma districts. Private operator: Liquid Intelligent Technologies (LIT) Zambia. ZICTA universal service coverage obligation USO/2024/003.

Name: Zamtel Smart City PPP Token – Lusaka Digital Infrastructure (ZMW 1.1 billion)
Description: PPP token for the Lusaka Smart City Phase I digital infrastructure programme: 2,400 smart street light nodes with IoT sensors, city-wide CCTV (850 cameras), traffic management system, and disaster management communications network. Technology partner: Huawei–NEC consortium. Lusaka City Council concession LCC/SMART/2024/001. ZPPA ref ZPPA/PPP/LCC/2024/008.

Name: Zamtel Universal Service PPP – Schools & Clinics Digital Access Programme
Description: PPP token for the Smart Zambia Institute Schools and Clinics Digital Access Programme: fibre connectivity and ICT labs for 450 secondary schools and 280 rural health centres. Private co-funder: Microsoft Africa (tech and training). USAID $5M e-education co-financing. MOE and MoH procurement ref MOE/ICT/2024/012. ZICTA USO ref USO/2024/005.

Name: Zamtel National Fibre Backbone – 3,200 km Optic Network Asset
Description: Tokenised national fibre optic backbone infrastructure asset: 3,200 km of aerial ADSS and buried armoured fibre cable along TAZARA rail and Zambia Railways corridors. Connects all 10 provincial capitals and 12 major border crossings. Valued by independent assessor Analysys Mason at replacement cost plus 15-year useful life DCF. ZICTA network registration NRI-2018-0045.

Name: Zamtel Lusaka Metro Fibre Ring – 840 km Underground Duct Network
Description: Tokenised metro fibre ring and underground duct infrastructure in Lusaka: 840 km of buried HDPE ducts containing 288-core fibre, serving CBD, industrial areas, government ministries, embassies and hospitals. Asset value based on 2024 independent replacement cost survey (ZMW 3.2M/km installed). ZICTA network registration NRI-2016-0012. Open access to licensed operators.

Name: Zamtel International Gateway – EASSY/SEACOM Cable Landing Station
Description: Tokenised international Internet gateway asset: Zamtel's EASSY (East Africa Submarine System) and SEACOM cable landing station access in Dar es Salaam, Tanzania, with onward fibre link to Lusaka (1,800 km TAZARA route). Provides Zambia's primary international bandwidth capacity (400 Gbps). Capacity lease valued on 10-year IRU NPV. ZICTA international gateway licence ZIC/IG/2018/001.

Name: Zamtel Lusaka North National Data Centre Infrastructure Asset
Description: Tokenised data centre infrastructure asset at Lusaka North Technology Park: 3,200 sq ft Tier II facility (2 MW IT load, N+1 UPS, N+1 cooling). Houses GRZ ministry servers, NHIMA health records, PACRA registry and Zamtel enterprise clients. Asset value based on replacement cost assessment. ZICTA data centre registration ZIC/DC/2020/003.

Name: Zamtel Government Guarantee – AfDB Telecom Facility (USD 45 million)
Description: GRZ sovereign guarantee instrument backing Zamtel's USD 45 million long-term loan from the African Development Bank (AfDB) under the Digital Zambia Programme. AfDB loan ref ADB/ZM/TELECOM/2022/001. GRZ guarantee issued by Ministry of Finance under the Loans and Guarantees (Authorisation) Act, Cap 366. Guarantee term: 2022–2034. MOF ref MOF-SG-0089.

Name: Zamtel Government Guarantee – China Exim Bank Facility (USD 72 million)
Description: GRZ sovereign guarantee backing Zamtel's USD 72 million concessional loan from China Exim Bank for 4G/LTE national rollout, fibre backbone and Zamtel data centre Phase II. China Exim loan ref CEXIM/ZM/2021/0045. GRZ guarantee under MOF authorisation. 15-year term, 2% p.a. concessional rate. Technology supply: Huawei Technologies. MOF guarantee ref MOF-SG-0067.

Name: Zamtel USAID DFC Partial Risk Guarantee (USD 20 million)
Description: US International Development Finance Corporation (DFC) Partial Risk Guarantee (PRG) covering 50% of principal on Zamtel's USD 40 million private debt financing from Standard Chartered Zambia and First National Bank Zambia. DFC PRG for rural broadband infrastructure. DFC ref DFC/ZM/PRG/2023/0012. Zamtel commitment to serving 500,000 rural internet subscribers by 2027.

Name: Zamtel KfW Development Finance Guarantee – Digital Infrastructure (EUR 18 million)
Description: KfW Development Bank (Germany) partial credit guarantee covering EUR 18 million subordinated debt for Zamtel's ICT skills training centres and digital inclusion programme. KfW loan agreement KFW/ZM/ICT/2024/001. GRZ co-guarantee with Ministry of Education. Supports TEVET digital skills programme for 25,000 Zambian youth annually across 12 provincial training hubs. MOF ref MOF-KFW-0023.
